texto: What women are concerned in is developing their own individuality, and hence they refuse to call any man master, be he husband or spiritual guide. Personal freedom is more precious to them than the protection of the best men. The women they envy are not those who are simply wives and mothers, but those who by honest intelligent work have attained distinction in any line of effort, and whose creed has been self-reliance.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Neith Boyce sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “What women are concerned in is developing their own individuality, and hence they refuse to call …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, independence, self-actualization, útil em momentos de termino e trabalho.
